Exponential Biological Technology

Figure 1. On a linear plot, Moore's law is an exponential. On this semi-log plot, where Moore's law is approximately linear, the rate of DNA synthesis and sequencing capacity are both increasing at least exponentially, which means that their real rate of increase is super exponential. For comparison, the approximate rate at which a single molecule of E. coli DNA Polymerase III replicates DNA is shown, referenced to an eight hour day. ABI synthesis and sequencing data, Gene Machines data, and Intel transistor data courtesy of those corporations. Pyrosequencing data courtesy of Mostafa Ronaghi at the Stanford Genome Technology Center.

The rates of DNA sequencing and synthesis can be thought of as measures of our ability to interact with biological systems.

 

HERE IS THE DRAFT ARTICLE: "The Pace of Biological Technology" (PDF format -- comments welcome).
